title: 🎙️ Community Tech & Governance
description: SD27, CRD, Williams Lake council, municipal networks, civic innovation - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: SD27, CRD, Williams Lake council, municipal networks, civic innovation
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Community Tech & Governance' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: municipal broadband and fiber projects in Cariboo communities, SD27 education technology and digital learning, civic tech platforms and open data initiatives, community-owned networks and ISPs, smart rural infrastructure (water, waste, transit), digital government services for rural areas, council decisions on technology spending, public Wi-Fi and connectivity programs, election technology and civic engagement platforms, rural innovation hubs, and how Williams Lake/Quesnel/100 Mile/CRD use technology for public services. Prefer articles that bridge governance AND technology in rural civic contexts. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [Boing Boing] VPN use surges in Australia after porn sites block users over age verification law
items.content_html:
When Australia begun implementing a British-style age verification law for accessing "adult" material online, porn sites began blocking Australian users rather than comply with the privacy-invading requirements. The result? A surge in VPN downloads.

Australian downloads of VPN apps have skyrocketed as one of the world's biggest pornography websites begins blocking Down Under users in protest of new age verification laws.

— Read the rest

The post VPN use surges in Australia after porn sites block users over age verification law appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [Boing Boing] 21 years on a remote island with no power grid, no building codes, and no regrets
items.content_html:
Lasqueti Island sits in British Columbia's Strait of Georgia, about 50 miles northwest of Vancouver. It's roughly the size of Manhattan but home to fewer than 500 people, and it has no connection to BC Hydro's power grid — residents generate their own electricity from solar panels, wind turbines, and micro-hydro systems, or they just go without. — Read the rest

The post 21 years on a remote island with no power grid, no building codes, and no regrets appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [The Verge] Ford is giving its commercial fleet business an AI makeover
items.content_html: Ford announced a new AI-powered service for its commercial fleet and telematics software customers called Ford Pro AI. The generative AI system analyzes data generated by commercial vehicles - including vehicle speed, seat belt activity, and engine health - and converts it into actionable items for fleet managers. The new system manifests as - what […]

items.title: [New Atlas] Bio-inspired robo-dolphin could soon be vacuuming oil off the sea's surface
items.content_html:
When it comes to systems for cleaning up marine oil spills, most of them simply float in place, waiting for the oil to come to them. A new robot, however, could proactively move through oil slicks – and it's inspired by both a dolphin and a sea urchin.

Continue Reading

Category: Robotics, Technology

Tags: RMIT University, Environment, Oil, Biomimicry
